name: fermatLastTheoremFor_of_five_le
text: `fermatLastTheoremFor_of_five_le : ÔêÇ p, p.Prime ÔåÆ 5 Ôëñ p ÔåÆ FermatLastTheoremFor p` (`Fermat/PrimeFive.lean`) ÔÇö proven from:

name: FreyPackage
text: `FreyPackage` + `FreyPackage.of_not_FermatLastTheoremFor_p_ge_5` + `FreyPackage.fermatLastTheoremFor_p_ge_5` (`Fermat/FreyPackage.lean`, adapted from the FLT project, Apache 2.0) ÔÇö a counterexample for prime `p ÔëÑ 5` normalizes to a Frey package (coprime, `a Ôëí 3 mod 4`, `b` even). Fully proven, no sorry

name: FreyPackage.freyCurve
text: `FreyPackage.freyCurve : WeierstrassCurve ÔäÜ` with `IsElliptic` instance, `╬ö`, `bÔéé`, `bÔéä`, `cÔéä`, `j`, and `FreyCurve.j_valuation_of_bad_prime` (`Fermat/FreyCurve.lean`, adapted from the FLT project). Fully proven, no sorry

name: FreyPackage.mazur
text: `FreyPackage.mazur` (`Fermat/FLT/FreyCurve/Mazur.lean`) ÔÇö the mod-p rep of the Frey curve is irreducible ÔÇö now (2026-07-16) from two explicit nodes in `Fermat/FLT/FreyCurve/MazurTorsion.lean` (own work), following Serre (Duke 1987, ┬ğ4.1):

name: FreyPackage.exists_torsion_embedding_of_not_isIrreducible
text: `FreyPackage.exists_torsion_embedding_of_not_isIrreducible` ÔÇö (2026-07-16) from the two nodes below: Serre's analysis produces full 2-torsion plus a rational point of order p on some curve; the `embedding_assembly` combines them into an injective Ôäñ/2 ├ù Ôäñ/2p via CRT

name: FreyPackage.exists_two_torsion_and_p_point_of_not_isIrreducible
text: `FreyPackage.exists_two_torsion_and_p_point_of_not_isIrreducible` ÔÇö (2026-07-16) from the disjunction node below plus the Frey 2-torsion

name: FreyPackage.exists_p_point_of_not_isIrreducible
text: `FreyPackage.exists_p_point_of_not_isIrreducible` ÔÇö (2026-07-16): the Minkowski input is discharged by the node below

name: minkowski_character_trivial
text: `minkowski_character_trivial` ÔÇö (2026-07-16) from the subgroup form below: the kernel is an open normal subgroup containing every inertia image (`Subgroup.map_le_iff_le_comap` + `ker (¤çÔêİres) = comap res (ker ¤ç)`), hence everything, so ¤ç = 1

name: open_normal_subgroup_eq_top_of_inertia_le
text: `open_normal_subgroup_eq_top_of_inertia_le` ÔÇö Minkowski, subgroup form: (2026-07-16) from the inertia dictionary below plus mathlib's discriminant theory (fixed field of the open normal kernel via the infinite Galois correspondence; finite Galois number field by `isOpen_iff_finite`/`normal_iff_isGalois`; `1 < finrank` from `H Ôëá Ôèñ`; `exists_not_isUnramifiedAt_int_of_isGalois` gives an everywhere-ramified prime `p`; going-up lifts it; the dictionary contradicts)

name: isUnramifiedAt_of_inertia_le_fixingSubgroup
text: `isUnramifiedAt_of_inertia_le_fixingSubgroup` ÔÇö THE inertia dictionary: (2026-07-16, compiled in ONE shot from the pre-verified chain: `liesOver_span_iff` for `LiesOver`, residue-field finiteness via the fraction-ring bridge, `card_inertia_eq_ramificationIdxIn`, `ramificationIdxIn_eq_ramificationIdx`, `ramificationIdx_eq_one_iff.mp`)

name: inertia_eq_bot_of_le_fixingSubgroup
text: `inertia_eq_bot_of_le_fixingSubgroup` ÔÇö the inertia transport (; whole chain now rests on the ONE local node below): the image of `localInertiaGroup q` fixing `L` pointwise trivializes `Q.inertia (L ÔëâÔéÉ[ÔäÜ] L)`. SPLIT EXECUTED (2026-07-16): the transport is from `exists_prime_over_inertia_eq_bot_of_le_fixingSubgroup` (SOME prime above `q` has trivial inertia ÔÇö 2026-07-16 via the VALUATION route, no decomposition groups and no henselian lifting: the chosen embedding `╬╣` carries `L` into the finite subextension `M = ÔäÜ_q(╬╣ L)` of `ÔäÜ_qßÁâ╦íßÁı`; `hle` + the `lift_map` put `M` inside the fixed field of the local inertia; the local node below then makes `q` a uniformizer of `­ØÆ¬_M`; pulling `­Øö¬_M` back along the ring hom `­ØôŞ L ÔåÆ ­ØÆ¬_M` induced by `╬╣` gives a prime `QÔéÇ Ôêï q` with `e(QÔéÇ|q) = 1` ÔÇö if `e Ôëá 1` then `q Ôêê QÔéÇ┬▓` (`ramificationIdx'_ne_one_iff`), so `q = q┬▓c` in `­ØÆ¬_M`, making `q` a unit in a proper ideal ÔÇö and `#I(QÔéÇ) = e = 1` closes via `card_inertia_eq_ramificationIdxIn`. Helper lemmas unconditionally: `asIdeal_toHeightOneSpectrumRingOfIntegersRat` (`v_q = span q` in `­ØôŞ ÔäÜ`) and `maximalIdeal_adicCompletionIntegers_eq_span` (`­Øö¬(Ôäñ_q╦å) = span q`, via `intValuation_singleton` + `maximalIdeal_eq_span_uniformizer`); rests on `maximalIdeal_map_eq_of_le_fixedField_localInertiaGroup` (`Deformations/RepresentationTheory/LocalInertiaFixedField.lean` ÔÇö 2026-07-16/17, Neukirch II.9.11 from scratch: a finite subextension `M/KßÁÑ` of `KßÁÑßÁâ╦íßÁı` fixed pointwise by `localInertiaGroup v` has `e(M/KßÁÑ) = 1`. Proof as planned: Galois closure `N/KßÁÑ`; `|I(N/KßÁÑ)| = e(N/KßÁÑ)` at each finite level via `card_inertia_eq_ramificationIdxIn`, tower multiplicativity of `e`, finite-level inertia surjectivity by counting, and profinite compactness lifting of finite-level inertia elements to `localInertiaGroup v`) and `inertia_eq_bot_of_exists_prime_over` (conjugacy propagation ÔÇö 2026-07-16: `exists_smul_eq_of_isGaloisGroup` gives transitivity on primes over `q`; the conjugation identity `g Ôêê inertia (¤â ÔÇó Q) Ôåö ¤âÔü╗┬╣g¤â Ôêê inertia Q` is a short definitional argument from `mem_inertia` + the pointwise-smul API `Ideal.IsPrime.smul`/`pointwise_smul_eq_comap`)

name: FreyPackage.exists_p_point_of_not_isIrreducible_of_minkowski
text: `FreyPackage.exists_p_point_of_not_isIrreducible_of_minkowski` ÔÇö (2026-07-17) from the stable-line dichotomy leaf, the Galois descent for points, and the V├®lu quotient leaf (all `MazurTorsion.lean`)

name: FreyPackage.stable_line_dichotomy_of_not_isIrreducible
text: `FreyPackage.stable_line_dichotomy_of_not_isIrreducible` ÔÇö (2026-07-17) from the semistability leaf below + the character bookkeeping: the stable line (`exists_stable_line_of_not_isIrreducible`) carries unit-valued characters (`exists_subCharacter`/`exists_quotCharacter`, the scalar-action-on-rank-1 argument `exists_unit_character_of_finrank_one`), with `det = ¤çÔéü¤çÔéé` (`det_eq_subCharacter_mul_quotCharacter` via `LinearMap.det_eq_det_mul_det`) `= ¤ë╠ä` (the det node + `cyclotomicCharacterModL_eq_toZMod`); kernels are open (they contain the open kernel of ¤ü, `isOpen_setOf_galoisRep_eq_one` + `Subgroup.isOpen_mono`); Minkowski (hmink) kills the everywhere-unramified character; `¤çÔéü = 1` fixes a nonzero `wÔéÇ Ôêê W` (a fixed point of exact order `p`), `¤çÔéé = 1` trivializes the quotient action. SPELLING GOTCHAS (all resolved): (a) quotient triviality must be phrased via `W.mkQ`, not `¤ü g v ÔêÆ v Ôêê W` (HSub instance search sticks); (b) `P.freyCurve`-instantiated nTorsion carries `Rat.commRing` vs the `Field.toCommRing` spelling baked into `galoisRep`'s codomain ÔÇö defeq at DEFAULT transparency but NOT at instance transparency, so `letI`/`haveI` instances for the local spelling are invisible to TC search against the baked spelling; the cure is general-`V` lemmas whose instance binders are pinned by unification with the `¤übar` argument (pass `¤übar` FIRST, extra finiteness as a plain hypothesis, never an instance binder)

name: FreyPackage.subquotient_character_unramified
text: `FreyPackage.subquotient_character_unramified` ÔÇö (2026-07-17): away from `{2, p}` the whole representation kills inertia (`FreyCurve.torsion_isUnramified`, transported by the new generic-`K` bridge `character_localInertia_le_ker_of_isUnramifiedAt` + `Rat.subsingleton_ringHom`/`convert using 5` to reconcile the local-vs-generic `algebraMap` spellings ÔÇö the local ÔäÜ-spelling and `toLocal`'s generic one are NOT defeq-bridgeable because `Field.absoluteGaloisGroup.map` is unexposed; ring homs out of `ÔäÜ` are unique, so propositional bridging works); the unipotent-scalar lemmas (`subCharacter_eq_one_of_sq_eq_zero`, `quotCharacter_eq_one_of_sq_eq_zero`, ) turn `(¤ü¤âÔêÆ1)┬▓ = 0` into character-triviality

name: FreyPackage.inertia_two_unipotent
text: `FreyPackage.inertia_two_unipotent` ÔÇö (2026-07-17): the Frey curve has multiplicative reduction at `2` (`freyCurve_hasMultiplicativeReduction_at_two`, ), and the pointwise Tate unipotence leaf below transports through `map_mem_inertiaSubgroup_of_mem_localInertiaGroup`, the `(AÔêÆ1)┬▓ = A┬ÀA ÔêÆ A ÔêÆ A + 1` End-expansion (pointwise via `abel`), and the show-cast `Ôüä`-ambient collapse. SPELLING NOTE: a direct `exact` across the generic-vs-`Rat` `algebraMap` spellings is impossible (unexposed `IsAlgClosed.lift`); the working recipe is atom-level `rfl`-bridges (`hb`) for the representation-vs-`Point.map` steps plus `convert hp using 8` with closers `rfl`, `Subsingleton.elim`, and `congrArg` of `Field.absoluteGaloisGroup.map` (hom-level AND `¤â`-applied) over `Rat.subsingleton_ringHom`

name: WeierstrassCurve.torsion_unipotent_of_multiplicative_reduction
text: `WeierstrassCurve.torsion_unipotent_of_multiplicative_reduction` (`FreyCurve/Semistable.lean`, stated 2026-07-17) ÔÇö pointwise Tate unipotence: multiplicative reduction at `q Ôëá p` (`q = 2` allowed, no `p Ôêú v(j)`) makes every inertia element at a valuation subring over `Ôäñ_(q)` act with `¤â(¤âP) ÔêÆ ¤âP ÔêÆ ¤âP + P = 0` on the `p`-torsion (to be closed against the Tate-uniformization leaves)

name: FreyPackage.subquotient_character_unramified_at_p
text: `FreyPackage.subquotient_character_unramified_at_p` (stated 2026-07-17) ÔÇö flat/ordinary at `p`: one of the two characters is unramified at `p` itself (connected-├®tale sequence in the ordinary/ multiplicative case; supersingular excluded by reducibility)

name: FreyPackage.exists_quotient_curve_point
text: `FreyPackage.exists_quotient_curve_point` (stated 2026-07-17) ÔÇö the V├®lu quotient leaf: a stable line with trivial quotient action produces `E'/ÔäÜ` with full rational 2-torsion and a rational `p`-point (quotient by the rational subgroup; quantified over Weierstrass models)

name: WeierstrassCurve.exists_point_eq_baseChange_of_fixed
text: `WeierstrassCurve.exists_point_eq_baseChange_of_fixed` ÔÇö (2026-07-17): Galois descent for points: a point of `E(ÔäÜ╠ä)` fixed by every `¤â Ôêê ╬ôÔäÜ` is the base change of a rational point (`InfiniteGalois.mem_range_algebraMap_iff_fixed` on the coordinates + `baseChange_nonsingular` descent + `Point.map_injective` order transfer)

name: exists_stable_line_of_not_isIrreducible
text: `exists_stable_line_of_not_isIrreducible` (`Chebotarev.lean`, 2026-07-16) ÔÇö a non-irreducible 2-dim mod-Ôäô rep has a Galois-stable line

name: FreyPackage.freyCurve_two_torsion_embedding
text: `FreyPackage.freyCurve_two_torsion_embedding` ÔÇö (2026-07-16): the Frey model has rational 2-torsion points (0, 0) and (aßÁû/4, ÔêÆaßÁû/8) (the quadratic factors as (x ÔêÆ aßÁû/4)(x + bßÁû/4)); they are distinct, of order 2 (fixed by negation `negY`), and generate an injective (Ôäñ/2)┬▓ ÔåÆ+ E(ÔäÜ) via two `ZMod.lift`s and a coprod, with injectivity by the four-element case analysis

name: embedding_assembly
text: `embedding_assembly` ( 2026-07-16): in an abelian group, an injective (Ôäñ/2)┬▓ and an element of order exactly p (odd prime) assemble into an injective Ôäñ/2 ├ù Ôäñ/2p (`ZMod.chineseRemainder`; the parts are separated by the coprime annihilators 2 and p)

name: WeierstrassCurve.mazur_torsion_bound
text: `WeierstrassCurve.mazur_torsion_bound` ÔÇö Mazur's torsion theorem, weak form: no elliptic curve over ÔäÜ has a subgroup of rational points Ôëà Ôäñ/2 ├ù Ôäñ/2p for p ÔëÑ 5 (primality dropped as unneeded) ÔÇö now (2026-07-16) from the faithful classification below: images of an injective hom from the finite group Ôäñ/2 ├ù Ôäñ/2p are torsion (finite additive order), the hom corestricts into the torsion submodule, and 4p ÔëÑ 20 > 16 ÔëÑ the order of every group in Mazur's list (`Nat.card` comparison)

name: WeierstrassCurve.mazur_classification
text: `WeierstrassCurve.mazur_classification` ÔÇö Mazur's torsion theorem, stated faithfully: the torsion submodule (`Submodule.torsion Ôäñ E(ÔäÜ)`) is Ôëâ+ to one of the fifteen groups Ôäñ/n (n Ôêê {1,ÔÇĞ,10,12}) or Ôäñ/2 ├ù Ôäñ/2m (m Ôêê {1,ÔÇĞ,4}). Mazur, Publ. Math. IH├ëS 47 (1977); Invent. Math. 44 (1978)

name: FreyPackage.galoisRep_not_irreducible
text: `FreyPackage.galoisRep_not_irreducible` (B4, `Fermat/PrimeFive.lean`) ÔÇö now (2026-07-16) from two explicit nodes, mirroring the FLT project's hardly-ramified plan (their B5/B6, stated in Lean here before upstream):

name: FreyCurve.torsion_isHardlyRamified
text: `FreyCurve.torsion_isHardlyRamified` (`GaloisRepresentation/HardlyRamified/Frey.lean`) ÔÇö now (2026-07-16) as the structure constructor applied to the four defining conditions, each an explicit node in `HardlyRamified/FreyConditions.lean` (own work):

name: FreyCurve.torsion_det
text: `FreyCurve.torsion_det` ÔÇö det ¤ü╠ä = mod-p cyclotomic character ÔÇö now (2026-07-16) via the Weil pairing route (`EllipticCurve/WeilPairing.lean`, own work):

name: WeilPairing.exists_weilPairing
text: `WeilPairing.exists_weilPairing` ÔÇö the Weil pairing: (2026-07-17) as the coordinate determinant form in a `finBasis` (`#E[p] = p┬▓` Ôş╣ rank 2), Galois-scaled by `det ¤ü` (`pairing_map_eq_det_smul`) = the cyclotomic character by the det node below

name: det_galoisRep_eq_cyclotomic
text: `det_galoisRep_eq_cyclotomic` ÔÇö (2026-07-17): `det ¤ü╠ä` and `¤ç╠ä` are continuous conjugation-invariant `ZMod p`-valued functions on `╬ô ÔäÜ` (continuity of `det Ôêİ ¤ü` from discreteness of `End` via `discreteTopology_moduleTopology`; `¤ç╠ä`-continuity in `Chebotarev.lean`); they agree at `Frob_q` for almost all `q` (the leaf below + `cyclotomicCharacterModL_globalFrob`, ), and the Frobenius conjugacy classes are dense (`dense_conjClasses_globalFrob`, rooted in the Chebotarev node), so the closed agreement set is everything. Bridge `cyclotomicCharacterModL_eq_toZMod` (`¤ç╠ä = toZMod Ôêİ ¤ç`) via `modularCyclotomicCharacter.unique` + `toZMod_eq_ringEquivCongr_comp_toZModPow`

name: det_galoisRep_globalFrob
text: `det_galoisRep_globalFrob` (`EllipticCurve/WeilPairing.lean`, stated 2026-07-17): Frobenius determinant at good primes ÔÇö away from a finite set of places, `det ¤ü╠ä(Frob_q) = q mod p` (the point-counting/Weil computation over the reduced curve; route: NOS reduction injectivity + Frobenius-isogeny degree). - (the other root of this derivation is the Chebotarev node `exists_frobenius_conj_mem_coset`, listed under the ChebotarevÔÇôBrauerÔÇôNesbitt cone.)

name: WeilPairing.pairing_map_eq_det_smul
text: `WeilPairing.pairing_map_eq_det_smul` + `WeilPairing.det_eq_of_conj` ÔÇö : on a 2-dimensional space an alternating form transforms under any endomorphism by the determinant (basis + 2├ù2 computation), so scaling by `c` forces `det = c`

name: FreyCurve.torsion_isUnramified
text: `FreyCurve.torsion_isUnramified` ÔÇö unramified outside {2, p}: (2026-07-16) by the case split `q Ôêú abc` or not, from the two nodes below

name: FreyCurve.torsion_isUnramified_of_good
text: `FreyCurve.torsion_isUnramified_of_good` ÔÇö (2026-07-16) from the two `FreyCurve/Semistable.lean` nodes below

name: FreyPackage.freyCurve_hasGoodReduction_of_not_dvd
text: `FreyPackage.freyCurve_hasGoodReduction_of_not_dvd` (`FreyCurve/Semistable.lean`, own work): (2026-07-16) ÔÇö at odd `q Ôêñ abc` the Frey equation is `q`-integral (integrality via `freyCurveInt` + `FreyCurve.map`) with unit discriminant `(abc)^{2p}/2Ôü©` (numerator and denominator prime to `q`, hence a unit of `Ôäñ_(q)`; adic valuation `1` via `mker_valuation_eq_isUnitSubmonoid`), hence minimal (valuation `1` is maximal among integral models) with good reduction over `Ôäñ_(q) = Localization.AtPrime`

name: WeierstrassCurve.isUnramifiedAt_of_hasGoodReduction
text: `WeierstrassCurve.isUnramifiedAt_of_hasGoodReduction` (`FreyCurve/Semistable.lean`, own work): (2026-07-17) ÔÇö the NOS local-global glue: good reduction at `q Ôëá p` gives `IsUnramifiedAt q` for the mod-`p` torsion rep, closed against the NOS leaf below via the embedded-valuation-subring machinery + spelling bridge (`map_mem_inertiaSubgroup_of_mem_localInertiaGroup`, `LocalInertiaFixedField.lean`)

name: torsion_unramified_of_good_reduction
text: `torsion_unramified_of_good_reduction` (`KnownIn1980s/EllipticCurves/GoodReduction.lean`): 2026-07-17, AXIOM-CLEAN ÔÇö the NOS easy direction for odd primes `n` (hypotheses `(hp : n.Prime) (hodd : Odd n)` added and threaded through `isUnramifiedAt_of_hasGoodReduction` and `FreyCurve.torsion_isUnramified_of_good`; Frey callers supply oddness from `P.hp5`). Proof: inertia fixes residues (`MonoidHom.mem_ker` + `toRingAut` + `ResidueField.residue_smul`); a torsion point `some x y h` has integral coordinates (`torsion_abscissa_mem`/`torsion_ordinate_mem`, Cassels); `¤âP` is again `n`-torsion with the same residues, so `torsion_abscissa_residue_ne` (separable reduction of `pre╬¿'ÔéÖ`) forces `¤âx = x` and `torsion_ordinate_eq_of_residue_eq` (the `¤êÔéé`-B├®zout y-quadratic argument) forces `¤ây = y`; `congr 1` closes the `Point.some` equality via `Affine.Point.map_some _ h`

name: FreyCurve.torsion_isUnramified_of_multiplicative
text: `FreyCurve.torsion_isUnramified_of_multiplicative` ÔÇö (2026-07-16) from the arithmetic (`freyCurve_hasMultiplicativeReduction_of_dvd` + `j_valuation_of_bad_prime`) and the Tate glue node below

name: FreyPackage.freyCurve_hasMultiplicativeReduction_of_dvd
text: `FreyPackage.freyCurve_hasMultiplicativeReduction_of_dvd` (`FreyCurve/Semistable.lean`, own work): (2026-07-16) ÔÇö at odd `q Ôêú abc` the equation is `q`-integral, `cÔéä = c^{2p} - (ab)^p` is prime to `q` (pairwise coprimality forces exactly one of `ab`, `c` divisible by `q`), so `v(cÔéä) = 1` (minimality by the unit-`cÔéä` KrausÔÇôLaska criterion) while `╬ö = (abc)^{2p}/2Ôü©` lies in the maximal ideal

name: WeierstrassCurve.isUnramifiedAt_of_hasMultiplicativeReduction
text: `WeierstrassCurve.isUnramifiedAt_of_hasMultiplicativeReduction` (`FreyCurve/Semistable.lean`, own work): (2026-07-17) ÔÇö the Tate glue: multiplicative reduction at odd `q Ôëá p` with `p Ôêú v_q(j)` Ôş╣ `IsUnramifiedAt q`, by the same embedded-subring transport as the good case, against the new pure-Tate content leaf below

name: FreyCurve.torsion_isFlat
text: `FreyCurve.torsion_isFlat` ÔÇö flat at p: (2026-07-16) by the case split `p Ôêú abc` or not, from the two nodes below

name: WeierstrassCurve.isFlatAt_of_hasGoodReduction
text: `WeierstrassCurve.isFlatAt_of_hasGoodReduction` (`FreyCurve/Semistable.lean`, own work): (2026-07-17) ÔÇö good reduction at `p` Ôş╣ `IsFlatAt p` for the mod-`p` torsion rep, from the leaf below plus the shared flat transport `GaloisRep.isFlatAt_of_dvr_package` (see its own subtree entry under the multiplicative case)

name: torsion_flat_of_good_reduction
text: `torsion_flat_of_good_reduction` (`KnownIn1980s/EllipticCurves/Flat.lean`, 2026-07-16): good reduction over a DVR makes the `n`-torsion a finite flat group scheme (Hopf algebra, finite flat, ├®tale generic fibre, equivariant points isomorphism). Plus the division-polynomial node `isCoprime_╬Ğ_╬¿Sq` ÔÇö restated for fields and directly (2026-07-17; the former `resultant_╬Ğ_╬¿Sq` node was DELETED, see the session-6 log)

name: FreyCurve.torsion_isFlat_of_multiplicative
text: `FreyCurve.torsion_isFlat_of_multiplicative` ÔÇö (2026-07-16) from the arithmetic (`freyCurve_hasMultiplicativeReduction_of_dvd` at `q := p` + `j_valuation_of_bad_prime`) and the glue node below

name: WeierstrassCurve.isFlatAt_of_hasMultiplicativeReduction
text: `WeierstrassCurve.isFlatAt_of_hasMultiplicativeReduction` (`FreyCurve/Semistable.lean`, own work): (2026-07-17) ÔÇö the peu-ramifi├®e glue: multiplicative reduction at `p` with `p Ôêú v_p(j)` Ôş╣ `IsFlatAt p`, from the new content leaf below plus the shared flat transport

name: torsion_flat_of_multiplicative_reduction
text: `torsion_flat_of_multiplicative_reduction` (`FreyCurve/Semistable.lean`, stated 2026-07-17): multiplicative reduction over `Ôäñ_(p)` with `p Ôêú v_p(j)` produces a finite flat Hopf algebra over `Ôäñ_(p)` (├®tale generic fibre) whose `ÔäÜ╠ä`-points are `╬ô ÔäÜ`-equivariantly the `p`-torsion ÔÇö the peu-ramifi├®e package in the same DVR-`Ôêâ`-shape as the good-reduction leaf (Tate curve + Kummer theory content)

name: GaloisRep.isFlatAt_of_dvr_package
text: `GaloisRep.isFlatAt_of_dvr_package` (`Deformations/RepresentationTheory/FlatProlongation.lean`) ÔÇö the shared flat transport, (2026-07-17): a DVR package over `Ôäñ_(q)` with equivariant `WithConv`-points iso onto the rep's space (over a field `A`) gives `IsFlatAt q`. The open-ideal quantifier runs over the two ideals of `A`: `Ôèñ` via `hasFlatProlongationAt_of_subsingleton` ( ÔÇö trivial Hopf algebra `­ØÆ¬ßÁÑ`), `ÔèÑ` via the transport `HasFlatProlongationAt.of_addEquiv` across `M Ôëâ+ (A Ôğ© ÔèÑ) Ôèù[A] M` (`AlgEquiv.quotientBot` + `baseChange_tmul`)

name: FreyPackage.freyCurve_hasMultiplicativeReduction_at_two

name: WeierstrassCurve.isTameAtTwo_of_hasMultiplicativeReduction
text: `WeierstrassCurve.isTameAtTwo_of_hasMultiplicativeReduction` (stated in `FreyConditions.lean` for a general elliptic curve over ÔäÜ): the Tate glue at 2 ÔÇö multiplicative reduction at 2 and `p` odd give the rank-1 unramified quotient with character squaring to 1; to be closed against the quadratic-twist and Tate-uniformization (`exists_tateEquivSepClosure`) nodes

name: GaloisRepresentation.not_isIrreducible_of_isHardlyRamified
text: B5 `GaloisRepresentation.not_isIrreducible_of_isHardlyRamified` (`GaloisRepresentation/HardlyRamified/Reducible.lean`, own work) ÔÇö now (2026-07-16) from three explicit nodes in `HardlyRamified/Lift.lean` (own work), following Buzzard's 2026 EPSRC Lecture 4 (his B5a/B5b/B5c):

name: exists_hardlyRamifiedLift
text: B6a `exists_hardlyRamifiedLift` ÔÇö an irreducible hardly ramified mod-Ôäô rep (Ôäô ÔëÑ 5) lifts to a hardly ramified Ôäô-adic rep over the integers `O` of a finite extension of `ÔäÜ_Ôäô` (bundled in `structure HardlyRamifiedLift`: `O` + framed rep + reduction map + Frobenius-charpoly compatibility). Deformation theory / modularity lifting without residual modularity

name: residual_charFrob_eq
text: B6bc `residual_charFrob_eq` ÔÇö the residual Frobenius charpolys of a liftable rep are those of `1 Ôèò ¤ç╠ä` (`X┬▓ ÔêÆ (q+1)X + q`) ÔÇö now (2026-07-16) from the faithful split ( from the FLT project's newer layer):

name: IsHardlyRamified.mem_isCompatible
text: B6b `IsHardlyRamified.mem_isCompatible` (`HardlyRamified/Family.lean`, ; conclusion named `IsInHardlyRamifiedFamily` as a marked ) ÔÇö a hardly ramified Ôäô-adic rep lives in a compatible family (`GaloisRepFamily.lean`, defs, ) all of whose odd members are hardly ramified. STRENGTHENED (2026-07-16): the package now records injectivity of the coefficient-ring embeddings into `ÔäÜ╠ä_p` ÔÇö an audit of the glue's proof skeleton showed the upstream statement is too weak for the charpoly descent (algebraMap from a domain to a field need not be injective); true for the intended subrings of `ÔäÜ╠ä_p`

name: residual_charFrob_eq_of_family
text: `residual_charFrob_eq_of_family` (own work, `Lift.lean`) ÔÇö compatibility BOOKKEEPING ÔÇö now (2026-07-16): extract the 3-adic member via the number-field embedding; its charpoly at Frob_q is `X┬▓ ÔêÆ (1+q)X + q` by B6c's trace + the cyclotomic determinant at Frobenius + the 2-dim reconstruction (generalized to comm rings); transport through baseChange-conj to the family, descend to the coefficient field by injectivity of the embedding, ride compatibility to the Ôäô-adic member, descend to `O` by the strengthened-B6b injectivity, and reduce through `charFrob_compat`. Exceptional set: `SÔéÇ Ôê¬ {2-place, 3-place}`. Consumes B6c and the Ôäô-adic Frobenius-value node. AUDIT RESTATEMENT (2026-07-16): the conclusion (and B6bc's, and the ChebotarevÔÇôBrauerÔÇôNesbitt hypothesis) now carries a finite exceptional set `S` of places ÔÇö the family's `isCompatible` only pins charpolys outside an unspecified finite set, so the `ÔêÇ q Ôêë {2,3,Ôäô}` form was unprovable; the density argument absorbs any finite `S` (new bridge: `toHeightOneSpectrumRingOfIntegersRat_injective`, distinct primes give distinct places, so a finite set of places excludes only finitely many primes in the auxiliary-prime selection). Proof ingredients consumed:

name: IsHardlyRamified.three_adic
text: B6c `IsHardlyRamified.three_adic` (`HardlyRamified/Threeadic.lean`, ) ÔÇö a 3-adic hardly ramified rep has trace(Frob_q) = 1 + q for q ÔëÑ 5

name: IsHardlyRamified.mod_three
text: `IsHardlyRamified.mod_three` (`ModThree.lean`) ÔÇö : a mod-3 hardly ramified rep has a ╬ôÔäÜ-equivariant surjection onto the trivial character; B6c's eventual proof lifts this 3-adically. Children:

name: mod_three_reducible

name: mod_three_of_stable_line
text: `mod_three_of_stable_line` ÔÇö (2026-07-17) from the new local leaf below + the (now field-generic) character bookkeeping of `MazurTorsion.lean`: quotient character `¤çÔéé` of the leaf's line, trivial-on-`ker ¤ü` (unipotent scalar lemma), open kernel (`isOpen_setOf_galoisRep_eq_one`), unramified outside `{2,3}` from `IsHardlyRamified.isUnramified` via the generic bridge + `Rat.subsingleton_ringHom` convert; `minkowski_character_trivial` (target-generalized to any group) kills `¤çÔéé`; `¤Ç` := coordinate of the rank-1 quotient

name: exists_line_with_locally_unramified_quotCharacter
text: `exists_line_with_locally_unramified_quotCharacter` (`ModThree.lean`, stated 2026-07-17) ÔÇö the LOCAL leaf: a reducible mod-3 hardly ramified rep has a stable line whose quotient character is unramified at `2` AND `3` (flat connected-├®tale analysis at `3` incl. the Serre swap; tame quadratic condition at `2`). - NB the lift structure gained an `IsModuleTopology Ôäñ_[Ôäô] O` field (statement strengthening of B6a's conclusion, true for integers of finite extensions of ÔäÜ_Ôäô; required by B6b)

name: not_isIrreducible_of_charFrob_eq
text: `not_isIrreducible_of_charFrob_eq` ÔÇö Chebotarev + BrauerÔÇô Nesbitt ÔÇö now (2026-07-16, `Chebotarev.lean` + proof in `Lift.lean`): the agreement set with `1 Ôèò ¤ç╠ä`'s charpolys is closed (module topology on `End` over `ZMod Ôäô` is discrete ; coefficient maps continuous) and contains the dense Frobenius conjugates, so BrauerÔÇôNesbitt applies. Children:

name: dense_conjClasses_globalFrob
text: `dense_conjClasses_globalFrob` ÔÇö Chebotarev density, topological form ÔÇö now (2026-07-16) by the profinite limit argument (: cosets of fixing subgroups of finite subextensions are a neighborhood basis, `krullTopology_mem_nhds_one_iff`; the finite-level statement puts a Frobenius conjugate in every coset):

name: exists_frobenius_conj_mem_coset
text: `exists_frobenius_conj_mem_coset` ÔÇö Chebotarev, finite level: for every finite subextension `E` of `K╠ä/K` and every `¤â`, the coset `¤â┬ÀGal(K╠ä/E)` contains a conjugate of a `globalFrob v` with `v Ôêë S` (existence form of Chebotarev for the Galois closure of `E/K`)

name: not_isIrreducible_of_charpoly_eq
text: `not_isIrreducible_of_charpoly_eq` ÔÇö BrauerÔÇôNesbitt, 2-dim mod-Ôäô instance ÔÇö SORRY-FREE (2026-07-16): CayleyÔÇôHamilton turns the charpoly hypothesis into `(¤üg ÔêÆ 1)(¤üg ÔêÆ ¤ç╠äg) = 0`; on `H := ker ¤ç╠ä` every element is unipotent, Kolchin gives a nonzero `H`-fixed space, stable under ╬ô (H normal); if proper it refutes irreducibility (`not_isIrreducible_of_invariant_submodule`, via `Subrepresentation`); if everything, the image commutes (commutators die in H) and the common-eigenvector lemma yields an invariant line. Children (both proven):

name: BrauerNesbitt.exists_fixed_of_unipotent
text: `BrauerNesbitt.exists_fixed_of_unipotent` ÔÇö Kolchin, 2-dim: a group of unipotent endomorphisms has a common nonzero fixed vector ÔÇö (2026-07-16, ). Route: matrix helpers `trace_eq_zero_and_det_eq_zero_of_sq_eq_zero` (square-zero 2├ù2 has zero trace/det, entry computation) and `sandwich_of_det_eq_zero` (rank-one identity `NÔéÇNNÔéÇ = tr(NNÔéÇ)ÔÇóNÔéÇ`); unipotency of `¤ü g`, `¤ü gÔéÇ`, `¤ü (g gÔéÇ)` forces `tr(NNÔéÇ) = 0`, so `nÔéÇ (¤ü g ÔêÆ 1) nÔéÇ = 0`; the line `range nÔéÇ = ker nÔéÇ` is preserved with square-zero scalar action, hence fixed pointwise

name: BrauerNesbitt.exists_common_eigenvector_of_commuting
text: `BrauerNesbitt.exists_common_eigenvector_of_commuting` ÔÇö a commuting family annihilated by split quadratics on a 2-dim space has a common eigenvector ÔÇö (2026-07-16, ): all-scalar case is trivial; otherwise a non-scalar member's eigenspace `ker (fÔéÇ ÔêÆ a)` is nonzero (else `fÔéÇ ÔêÆ b = 0` by injectivity), proper, hence 1-dimensional, preserved by commutativity, and its generator is the common eigenvector

name: cyclotomicCharacterModL_globalFrob
text: `cyclotomicCharacterModL_globalFrob` ÔÇö ¤ç╠ä(Frob_q) = q for q Ôëá Ôäô ÔÇö now (2026-07-16) by mod-Ôäô reduction (`cyclotomicCharacter.spec` at n = 1 + `modularCyclotomicCharacter.unique`) from:

name: cyclotomicCharacter_globalFrob
text: `cyclotomicCharacter_globalFrob` ÔÇö the Ôäô-adic cyclotomic character evaluates to q at `globalFrob q` (q Ôëá Ôäô) ÔÇö now (2026-07-16, the hardest assembly of the session): `lift_map` transports the action to `ÔäÜ_qßÁâ╦íßÁı`; `Ôäô^k`-th roots of unity are integral (`IsIntegral.of_pow`); `apply_of_pow_eq_one` at the maximal ideal of the integral closure gives the q-power action (exponent = q by the residue node, side condition by the unit node); descend by injectivity of the chosen embedding (forcing the adic-completion algebra instance against the `ratAlgebra` diamond); conclude by `modularCyclotomicCharacter.unique` at every level and `PadicInt.ext_of_toZModPow`. Serves the glue at Ôäô = 3 and ChebBN at Ôäô. Children: DERIVATION MAPPED (2026-07-16), all ingredients in-tree: (i) equivariance of `absoluteGaloisGroup.map` along the chosen embedding ÔÇö ALREADY in the tree as `Field.absoluteGaloisGroup.lift_map` (`AbsoluteGaloisGroup.lean`), and `AlgebraicClosure.map` is definitionally `IsAlgClosed.lift` (`Deformations/Lemmas.lean`); (ii) `natCard_residue_quotient_toHeightOneSpectrum` ÔÇö (2026-07-16): the contraction of the maximal ideal is the maximal ideal (`Ideal.IsMaximal.under` on the integral closure + local uniqueness); transport to `Ôäñ_[q]` by `adicCompletionIntegers.padicIntEquiv` (maximal ideals correspond via `Ideal.comap_symm`), the `Ôäñ_[p]` residue count is `p` (`toZMod` surjective with kernel `­Øö¬`), and `natGenerator (q-place) = q` via `span_natGenerator` and the `ringOfIntegersEquiv` bridge. WITH THIS, `cyclotomicCharacter_globalFrob` and its mod-Ôäô corollary are UNCONDITIONALLY ([propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]); the ChebotarevÔÇôBrauerÔÇôNesbitt chain now rests on the single leaf `exists_frobenius_conj_mem_coset`; (iiÔÇ▓) `isUnit_natCast_adicCompletionIntegers` ÔÇö (2026-07-16): a valuation-subring unit is an element of valuation one; the completion's valuation restricts to the global one, which on integers is the `intValuation`, equal to one iff `p Ôêë v` ÔÇö i.e. `p Ôëá q` by `natCast_mem_toHeightOneSpectrum_iff`; (iii) `AlgHom.IsArithFrobAt.apply_of_pow_eq_one` ( `Frobenius.lean`): a Frobenius sends m-th roots of unity to their q-th powers when q Ôêñ m ÔÇö apply at m = Ôäô^k via `isArithFrobAt_adicArithFrob`; (iv) transport through the embedding and conclude by `cyclotomicCharacter.unique`-mod-Ôäô^k plus `PadicInt.ext_of_toZModPow`

name: n_torsion_finite

Statement specialized to separably closed characteristic-zero fields ÔÇö the only fields at which the tree uses it (`galoisRep` gained `[CharZero K]`). The former division-polynomial route (`TorsionFinite.lean` with nodes `eval_╬¿Sq_eq_zero_of_smul_eq_zero`, `╬¿Sq_ne_zero_of_charDvd`, covering arbitrary characteristic) is SUPERSEDED and removed ÔÇö the frontier shrinks by two nodes

name: n_torsion_card
text: `n_torsion_card` (= n┬▓ over sep. closed fields, `(n : k) Ôëá 0`) ÔÇö now (2026-07-16, `TorsionCard.lean`, own work): `card_torsionBy` by strong induction peeling off the minimal prime factor ÔÇö multiplication by `p := n.minFac` restricts to a surjection `E[n] ÔåÆ E[n/p]` with kernel `E[p]`, so Lagrange + the first isomorphism theorem give `#E[n] = p┬▓┬À(n/p)┬▓`; no CRT needed. Faithful leaves:

Rests on the four sharp nodes below

name: EllipticDivisibilitySequence.normEDS_sum_companion
text: `EllipticDivisibilitySequence.normEDS_sum_companion` ÔÇö the universal `(ÔİàsÔÇ▓)` identity `bc(WÔéÖÔéïÔéü┬▓WÔéÖÔéèÔéé + WÔéÖÔéïÔééWÔéÖÔéèÔéü┬▓) = WÔéÖÔéïÔéüWÔéÖWÔéÖÔéèÔéü(db+bÔüÁ) ÔêÆ WÔéÖ┬│b┬│c` for `W = normEDS b c d` over any ring ÔÇö pure EDS, no geometry; to be proven by Stange-style double parity descent through the two-parameter family `T(p,q)` (mathlib TODO `IsEllipticSequence normEDS`; descent certificates verified to exist, `scripts/eds/`)

name: TorsionCard.exists_point_x_smul
text: `TorsionCard.exists_point_x_smul` ÔÇö rational points in multiplication fibres over a separably closed field (where separability of `[n]` enters)

name: TorsionCard.prime_torsion_card
text: `TorsionCard.prime_torsion_card` ÔÇö (2026-07-17): the nonzero `p`-torsion is fibred over the roots of the division polynomial by the dictionary node; a shared counting skeleton (`key`) computes `#E[p] = 1 + m┬Àdeg g` for a separable `g` cutting out the torsion `x`-coordinates with uniform `y`-fibre size `m`. Odd `p`: `g = pre╬¿' p` (separability node), `m = 2` (the `y`-fibre quadratic is separable since `╬¿ÔééSq(xÔéÇ) Ôëá 0` by the coprimality node), `1 + 2┬À(p┬▓-1)/2 = p┬▓`. `p = 2`: `g = ╬¿ÔééSq` (separability node), `m = 1` (the `y`-quadratic has discriminant `╬¿ÔééSq(xÔéÇ) = 0`, hence a unique root `-(aÔéüxÔéÇ+aÔéâ)/2`), `1 + 3 = 4`. infrastructure: `yQuad` (the `y`-fibre quadratic), `derivative_yQuad_sq_sub` (the characteristic-free discriminant identity `(ÔêéQ)┬▓ - 4Q = C (╬¿ÔééSq xÔéÇ)`), `yQuad_separable` (explicit B├®zout), `pointsAt` finset + biUnion counting

name: TorsionCard.separable_pre╬¿'
text: `TorsionCard.separable_pre╬¿'` ÔÇö separability of the odd-`p` division polynomial (disc companion identity, `disc(¤êÔéÜ) = ┬▒pßÁâ╬ößÁç`)

name: TorsionCard.isCoprime_╬¿ÔééSq_pre╬¿'
text: `TorsionCard.isCoprime_╬¿ÔééSq_pre╬¿'` ÔÇö (2026-07-17) from the dictionary node, NOT from EDS strong divisibility: a common root `╬▒` over `k╠ä` would carry a curve point `(╬▒, yÔéÇ)` that is `negY`-fixed (the discriminant identity `(ÔêéQ)┬▓ - 4Q = C (╬¿ÔééSq ╬▒) = 0` forces `2yÔéÇ + aÔéü╬▒ + aÔéâ = 0`, uniformly in the characteristic), hence `2`-torsion, and `p`-torsion by the dictionary ÔÇö trivial by `gcd(2,p) = 1`, contradicting affineness. Route: `┬¼coprime ÔåÆ gcd non-unit ÔåÆ root of the mapped gcd over `k╠ä` (`IsAlgClosed.exists_root`) ÔåÆ common root (`root_gcd_iff_root_left_right`)`, with the division polynomials transferred by `map_╬¿ÔééSq`/`map_pre╬¿'`

name: TorsionCard.separable_╬¿ÔééSq
text: `TorsionCard.separable_╬¿ÔééSq` ÔÇö (2026-07-17): disc = 16╬ö Ôëá 0 (`twoTorsionPolynomial_discr_ne_zero_of_isElliptic`), distinct roots over the algebraic closure (`Cubic.discr_ne_zero_iff_roots_nodup` + `nodup_roots_iff_of_splits`), separability descends along `Polynomial.separable_map`. Audits clean

name: WeierstrassCurve.galoisRep
text: `WeierstrassCurve.galoisRep` ÔÇö CONSTRUCTED (2026-07-16). The formerly sorry-d DATA is now the genuine representation: the Galois action on points (`Point.map`, via the `DistribMulAction` instance) restricted to the `n`-torsion and made `ZMod n`-linear (`AddMonoidHom.toZModLinearMap`). Continuity: the coordinates of the (finitely many, via `n_torsion_finite`) torsion points generate a finite extension `F/K`; the rep kills the open subgroup `Gal(KßÁâ╦íßÁı/F)` (`fixingSubgroup_isOpen`), so every fiber is a union of open cosets ÔÇö continuous into any topology on the target. `#print axioms`: sorryAx enters only through `n_torsion_finite`. Mazur/B4 are now statements about the REAL representation

name: group_theory_lemma
text: `group_theory_lemma` ÔÇö (2026-07-16) in `Fermat/FLT/EllipticCurve/TorsionCounting.lean` (own work, not ): structure theorem for finite abelian groups + torsion counting in `ZMod m` (`#torsionBy d (ZMod m) = gcd d m`, via the first isomorphism theorem) + multiset determination (each prime `q Ôêú n` occurs exactly `r` times, each exponent forced to `v_q(n)`) + CRT reassembly (`ZMod.equivPi`). Axioms: `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`

name: torsion_unipotent_of_split_multiplicative_adic
text: pointwise unipotence in the split case: the Tate uniformization witness feeds `tate_inertia_unipotent` at the local valuation subring, pulled back to `E(ÔäÜ╠ä)` along the equivariant embedding; the remaining content is the base-change instance identification of the two `╬®`-stage curve spellings.

name: WeierstrassCurve.exists_tateEquivSepClosure
text: Tate's uniformisation over a separable closure, now DERIVED from the choice-free Tate-curve uniformisation and Tate's variable-change theorem: the variable change is `k`-rational, so its base-changed point equivalence is Galois-equivariant, and the equivariance transports through the composite.

name: WeierstrassCurve.exists_variableChange_tateCurve
text: Tate's theorem (Silverman ATAEC V.5.3): a curve with split multiplicative reduction is a change of Weierstrass coordinates of the Tate curve of its Tate parameter.

name: WeierstrassCurve.tate_inertia_unipotent
text: local unipotence of inertia on Tate torsion, for ANY uniformization witness: the torsion class is represented by `u` with `u^p` a power of the Tate parameter; the parameter is base-field-fixed so `¤â(u)/u` is a `p`-th root of unity; inertia fixes it (residue characteristic prime to `p`).

name: WeierstrassCurve.torsion_unipotent_of_nonsplit_multiplicative_adic
text: the nonsplit half of the unipotence statement, assembled from the LOCAL nonsplit node `tate_inertia_unipotent_of_nonsplit` by the proven `ÔäÜ╠ä`-pullback glue (equivariant embedding + `Point.map` injectivity).

name: ValuationSubring.inertia_fixes_of_pow_eq
text: step (bÔÇ▓), the generalized root-fixing lemma: if `x^p` is a ¤â-fixed constant of the valuation subring with nonzero residue (and `p` prime to the residue characteristic), inertia fixes `x` ÔÇö `X^p ÔêÆ c` is separable over the residue field, so distinct roots have distinct residues.

name: WeierstrassCurve.tate_inertia_trivial
text: local TRIVIALITY of inertia on Tate torsion when the parameter is a `p`-th power up to units, for ANY uniformization witness: the torsion representative is recentred by a base-field unit power so that its `p`-th power is a ¤â-fixed unit constant of the valuation subring; `inertia_fixes_of_pow_eq` then fixes it outright.

name: valuation_eq_one_of_padicValRat_eq_zero
text: the reduced-fraction dictionary at `q`: a nonzero rational with vanishing `q`-adic `padicValRat` has `v_q`-adic valuation `1` (equal multiplicities on coprime numerator/denominator force both to vanish).

name: exists_unit_qUnit_mul_inv_pow_isUnit
text: the step-(d) witness: with `p Ôêú v_q(j)`, the Tate parameter of the completed base change is a unit of the completed integers times the `p`-th power of the image of `q^m` ÔÇö via `|q_E| = |j|Ôü╗┬╣`, the reduced-fraction dictionary, and the `Valued`/canonical valuation equivalence.

name: torsion_trivial_of_split_multiplicative_adic
text: pointwise inertia-TRIVIALITY in the split case with `p Ôêú v_q(j)`: the Tate uniformization witness feeds `tate_inertia_trivial` at the local valuation subring with the step-(d) witness, pulled back to `E(ÔäÜ╠ä)` along the equivariant embedding.

name: WeierstrassCurve.torsion_trivial_of_nonsplit_multiplicative_adic
text: the nonsplit half of the triviality statement, assembled from the LOCAL nonsplit node `tate_inertia_trivial_of_nonsplit` by the proven `ÔäÜ╠ä`-pullback glue; the `j`-hypothesis feeds through `map_j`.

name: WeierstrassCurve.torsion_trivial_of_multiplicative_reduction
text: pointwise inertia-triviality on torsion at multiplicative primes with `p Ôêú v_q(j)` ÔÇö the split/nonsplit case split; the local input to `isUnramifiedAt_of_hasMultiplicativeReduction`.

name: WeierstrassCurve.tate_inertia_unipotent_of_nonsplit
text: the LOCAL twist-transfer of nonsplit unipotence, now assembled: the enriched twist witness, the inertia-fixed embedding of the unramified quadratic extension, and the equivariant composite point equivalence transport `tate_inertia_unipotent` from the twisted minimal model.

name: WeierstrassCurve.tate_inertia_trivial_of_nonsplit
text: the LOCAL twist-transfer of nonsplit triviality, now assembled: as the unipotent analogue via `tate_inertia_trivial`, with the step-(d) witness applied to the twisted minimal model (same `j`-invariant through `variableChange_j` and `j_quadraticTwist`).

name: inertia_fixes_algHom_of_unramified_gen
text: inertia fixes every embedding of an unramified extension of `ÔäÜ_q╦å` (generator with monic integral lift of separable residue reduction): the master root-fixing lemma applied to the image of the generator, extended by the adjoin-Ôèñ equalizer argument.

name: ValuationSubring.inertia_fixes_root_of_separable_residue
text: the master root-fixing lemma: inertia fixes any `A`-integral root of a polynomial over `A` with base-field coefficients and separable residue reduction (subsumes the `X^p ÔêÆ 1` and `X^p ÔêÆ c` steps).

name: WeierstrassCurve.exists_tateCurveEquivSepClosure
text: the choice-free core of Tate's uniformisation: `E_q(╬®) Ôëà ╬®╦ú/q^Ôäñ` Galois-equivariantly ON THE NOSE, by the explicit series `X(u,q)`, `Y(u,q)` (whose Weierstrass equation is proven in `TateCurveConstruction`); glued over the finite subextensions of `╬®`.

name: WeierstrassCurve.exists_tateTorsionQuotient
text: the Tate valuation-exponent quotient on `p`-torsion, over any uniformization witness: a Galois-invariant surjection `E[p] ÔåÆ Ôäñ/p` by the exponent of the Tate parameter in the `p`-th power of any representative ÔÇö the quotient of `0 ÔåÆ ╬╝_p ÔåÆ E[p] ÔåÆ Ôäñ/p ÔåÆ 0`, carrying the trivial action in the split case. Step one of the tame-at-2 derivation.
